{"metadata":{"type":"page"},"title":"Volcano Queue DRA 资源配额支持 — 设计方案","url":"https://www.notion.so/31bca50a6ba48109a210e9e2461648d3","text":"Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://www.notion.so/31bca50a6ba48109a210e9e2461648d3 as of 2026-03-30T14:19:03.154Z: {"title":"Volcano Queue DRA 资源配额支持 — 设计方案"} # 背景与动机 Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) 是 Kubernetes 1.26+ 引入的新资源分配机制，用于替代传统的 Device Plugin 模型，支持更复杂的设备请求语义（整卡、vGPU consumable capacity、MIG 分区等）。 Volcano 的 capacity plugin 当前管理 CPU、Memory、ScalarResources 的 Queue 级别配额。为了支持 DRA 场景（尤其是 HAMi vGPU 等 consumable capacity 设备），需要扩展 Queue 配额管理能力。 ## DRA 核心资源模型 ```javascript ResourceSlice (per-node，DRA driver 发布) └─ devices[] ├─ attributes (CEL 可查询的设备属性) ├─ capacity (consumable 维度，如 cores/memory) └─ allowMultipleAllocations: true ← vGPU 场景关键字段 DeviceClass (cluster-scoped，驱动定义设备类型) ResourceClaimTemplate (namespace-scoped，用户定义请求模板) └─ spec.devices.requests[] ├─ exactly.deviceClassName ← 对应 DeviceClass ├─ exactly.count └─ capacity.requests ← consumable capacity 请求 ├─ cores: 30 └─ memory: "4Gi" ``` ## 关键特性 - 物理 GPU 以 `allowMultipleAllocations=true` 方式发布，同一块 GPU 可被多个 ResourceClaim 消费（类似 CPU core 的分时复用），一般配合Capability容量配额参数使用。 - Scheduler 保证所有 ResourceClaim 的 capacity 之和不超过设备总 capacity（使用kubernetes默认的DRA调度器逻辑） - **Queue quota 需追踪 capacity 维度，而非仅统计设备数量** ## DRA 使用模式分类
使用模式 代表场景 quota 需求
**Extended Resource 兼容模式** DRA driver 暴露 `nvidia.com/gpu` extended resource 已有 scalarResources，**无需改动**
**ResourceClaim 整卡模式** 整块 GPU 独占分配 需要 device count quota
**ResourceClaim consumable capacity** HAMi vGPU、MIG 分区 需要 capacity 维度 quota
--- # 设计方案对比 ## 方案 A：独立 `dra` 字段（当前方案） 在 QueueSpec 中新增独立的 `dra` 结构字段，与现有 `capability` 平行。 ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: ml-team spec: capability: cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # 新增独立 DRA 字段 dra: capability: gpu.nvidia.com: count: 8 hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io: count: 20 capacity: cores: "800" memory: "320Gi" deserved: hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io: count: 40 guarantee: hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io: count: 10 ``` **Go API 定义：** ```go type QueueSpec struct { // ... 现有字段 ... DRA *DRAQuota `json:"dra,omitempty"` } type DRAQuota struct { Capability map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"capability,omitempty"` Deserved map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"deserved,omitempty"` Guarantee map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"guarantee,omitempty"` } type DRAResourceQuota struct { Count int64 `json:"count"` Capacity map[string]resource.Quantity `json:"capacity,omitempty"` } ``` ### 优缺点分析
维度 评价
✅ 结构清晰 DRA 资源语义完整表达（count + capacity 关系显式）
✅ 扩展性好 可独立演进，不污染原有字段
❌ **配置割裂** 传统资源和 DRA 资源分开配置，认知负担重
❌ **弹性抢占复杂** `deserved`/`guarantee` 对 DRA 需要单独实现，无法复用 capacity plugin 现有逻辑
❌ **层级队列难继承** 父子队列的 DRA quota 继承需要全新代码路径
❌ **count 语义模糊** consumable capacity 场景下 `count` 含义不清晰
--- ## 方案 B：`deviceclass/` 前缀（新**方案**） 直接将 DeviceClass 名或加前缀嵌入到 `spec.capability` 的 ResourceList 中。将 `deviceclass/`作为前缀，明确指向 K8s `DeviceClass` 对象，语义更清晰。同时考虑 DRA Extended Resource 兼容路径，实现三种资源使用模式的统一覆盖。 ```yaml spec: capability: # ── 传统资源（不变）── cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # ── Device Plugin 模式（不变，scalarResources 路径）── "nvidia.com/gpu": "4" # ── DRA Extended Resource 兼容模式（不变）── # DRA driver 暴露 extended resource，复用同一 key，无感知切换 # "nvidia.com/gpu": "8" (同上，用户无需区分) # ── DRA ResourceClaim 整卡模式：deviceclass/ ── "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8" # ── DRA ResourceClaim consumable capacity：.deviceclass/ ── "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "800" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "320Gi" deserved: cpu: "60" memory: "120Gi" "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "4" "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "400" guarantee: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "100" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "40Gi" ``` ### Key 解析规则（三条，无歧义）
key 格式 匹配条件 语义
`deviceclass/` `HasPrefix("deviceclass/")` DeviceClass `` 的设备数量限制
`.deviceclass/` `Contains(".deviceclass/")` DeviceClass `` 的 `` capacity 维度限制
其他 兜底 传统资源 / Extended Resource（现有逻辑）
> **优先级**：先检查 `.deviceclass/`（capacity 格式），再检查 `deviceclass/`（count 格式），最后走传统路径。 ### 与方案 A/B 的核心区别
维度 方案 A（独立字段） 方案 B（`deviceclass/`）
配置统一性 ❌ 分裂 ✅
语义清晰度 ✅ 最高 ✅ 具体
解析复杂度 ✅ 低 ✅ 低
弹性抢占复用 ❌ 需新实现 ✅
层级队列继承 ❌ 需新实现 ✅
capacity plugin 改动量 ❌ 大 ✅ 小
key 可读性 ✅ ✅ 明确
Extended Resource 兼容 ✅（平行处理） ✅
--- # 推荐方案（方案 B）详细设计 ## DRA 使用路径全覆盖
使用场景 Queue capability 配置 解析路径 是否新增代码
普通 GPU（Device Plugin） `nvidia.com/gpu: "8"` 传统 ScalarResource ❌ 不需要
DRA Extended Resource 模式 `nvidia.com/gpu: "8"` 传统 ScalarResource ❌ 不需要
DRA 整卡（ResourceClaim count） `deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com: "8"` DeviceClass count ✅ 新增
DRA vGPU consumable cores `cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: "800"` DeviceClass capacity ✅ 新增
DRA vGPU consumable memory `memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: "320Gi"` DeviceClass capacity ✅ 新增
## Go 实现：Key 解析 ```go // resource_key.go const ( DeviceClassCountPrefix = "deviceclass/" DeviceClassCapacitySep = ".deviceclass/" ) type ResourceKeyType int const ( KeyTypeTraditional ResourceKeyType = iota // cpu, memory, nvidia.com/gpu KeyTypeDeviceClassCount // deviceclass/ KeyTypeDeviceClassCap // .deviceclass/ ) type ParsedResourceKey struct { Type ResourceKeyType DeviceClassName string // 用于 Count 和 Cap CapacityDim string // 仅 Cap 时有效 } func ParseResourceKey(r v1.ResourceName) ParsedResourceKey { s := string(r) // 优先检查 capacity 格式（含 ".deviceclass/"） if idx := strings.Index(s, DeviceClassCapacitySep); idx > 0 { return ParsedResourceKey{ Type: KeyTypeDeviceClassCap, DeviceClassName: s[idx+len(DeviceClassCapacitySep):], CapacityDim: s[:idx], } } // 再检查 count 格式（以 "deviceclass/" 开头） if strings.HasPrefix(s, DeviceClassCountPrefix) { return ParsedResourceKey{ Type: KeyTypeDeviceClassCount, DeviceClassName: strings.TrimPrefix(s, DeviceClassCountPrefix), } } return ParsedResourceKey{Type: KeyTypeTraditional} } // 构造工具函数（供 controller 和测试使用） func DeviceClassCountKey(dcName string) v1.ResourceName { return v1.ResourceName(DeviceClassCountPrefix + dcName) } func DeviceClassCapKey(dcName, dim string) v1.ResourceName { return v1.ResourceName(dim + DeviceClassCapacitySep + dcName) } ``` ## Go 实现：DRA 请求提取 ```go // 从 Pod 的 ResourceClaim 中提取 DRA 资源请求，翻译为统一 ResourceList func (cp *capacityPlugin) calcTaskDRAResources(task *api.TaskInfo) v1.ResourceList { result := v1.ResourceList{} for _, claimRef := range task.Pod.Spec.ResourceClaims { var tmpl *resourcev1.ResourceClaimTemplate if claimRef.ResourceClaimTemplateName != nil { // ResourceClaimTemplate 模式（主流） key := task.Namespace + "/" + *claimRef.ResourceClaimTemplateName tmpl = cp.claimTemplateCache[key] } else if claimRef.ResourceClaimName != nil { // 直接 ResourceClaim 引用（从 claim 读取 spec） // ... } if tmpl == nil { continue } for _, req := range tmpl.Spec.Spec.Devices.Requests { dcName := req.Exactly.DeviceClassName count := int64(1) if req.Exactly.Count != nil { count = *req.Exactly.Count } // 设备数量 countKey := DeviceClassCountKey(dcName) result[countKey] = addQuantity(result[countKey], count) // consumable capacity 维度 for dim, qty := range req.Exactly.Capacity.Requests { capKey := DeviceClassCapKey(dcName, string(dim)) result[capKey] = addQuantity(result[capKey], qty) } } } return result } ``` ## Capacity Plugin 集成（改动最小化） ```go // capacity plugin 核心改动仅在「请求量合并」阶段 // checkQuota 逻辑本身不变，因为 ResourceList 已统一 func (cp *capacityPlugin) Allocatable(queue *api.QueueInfo, task *api.TaskInfo) bool { // 合并传统资源请求 + DRA 资源请求 requested := task.Resreq.Clone() draResources := cp.calcTaskDRAResources(task) for k, v := range draResources { requested[k] = v } // 现有的 LessEqualWithDimension 逻辑直接复用 // capability 中现在同时包含传统 key 和 deviceclass/ key return requested.LessEqualWithDimension(queue.Queue.Spec.Capability, task) } ``` ## ResourceClaim 状态生命周期追踪 ```javascript ResourceClaim 状态 Volcano capacity plugin 动作 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Pending（未分配） 不计入 draAllocated Allocated + Reserved（绑定到 Pod） 计入 draAllocated Released（Pod 完成/删除） 从 draAllocated 移除 ``` 实现方式：Watch `ResourceClaim` 对象，在 `status.allocation` 字段变化时更新 Queue 的 DRA 已分配量。共享 ResourceClaim（多 Pod 引用同一 claim）按 **ResourceClaim 为单位计数一次**，与 K8s 原生 ResourceQuota 行为一致。 ## allocationMode: All 的处理（是否要支持） 当 ResourceClaim 使用 `allocationMode: All` 时（分配该 DeviceClass 下所有设备），在 ResourceSlice 中查询该 DeviceClass 对应的最大设备数（可能会导致计算额度超过队列quota配置），并计入 quota。 --- # 完整配置示例 ## 场景一：整卡 GPU 队列 ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: gpu-team spec: capability: cpu: "64" memory: "256Gi" # DRA 整卡，最多 8 块 "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8" deserved: "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "4" guarantee: "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "1" ``` ## 场景二：vGPU 队列（HAMi consumable capacity） ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: vgpu-team spec: capability: cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # 不限制消耗几块物理 GPU，仅限制总 capacity "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "800" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "320Gi" deserved: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "400" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "160Gi" guarantee: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "100" ``` ## 场景三：混合队列（整卡 + vGPU + 传统 Device Plugin） ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: ml-team spec: capability: # 传统资源 cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # Device Plugin GPU（老方式，继续支持） "nvidia.com/gpu": "4" # DRA 整卡 "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8" # DRA vGPU（同时限制数量上限和 capacity 总量） "deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "20" "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "800" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "320Gi" deserved: cpu: "60" memory: "120Gi" "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "4" "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "400" guarantee: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "100" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "40Gi" ``` --- # 与参考方案的横向对比 ## vs. Kueue KEP-2941
维度 Kueue 方案 Volcano 方案（本设计）
DeviceClass 映射 全局 ConfigMap `DeviceClassMappings`，DeviceClass → 逻辑名 直接用 `deviceclass/` key，无需额外配置层
consumable capacity ❌ 不支持，仅 count ✅ `.deviceclass/` 支持
ResourceClaim 支持 仅 Template，不支持直接 Claim 两者均支持
与现有 quota 集成 ResourceFlavor + ClusterQueue，结构独立 统一进 `capability` ResourceList，复用现有逻辑
弹性调度（deserved） BorrowingLimit / LendingLimit 与现有 `deserved`/`guarantee` 统一
## vs. K8s 原生 ResourceQuota K8s 原生 ResourceQuota 仅支持 `count/resourceclaims`（按对象数量统计），无法感知 capacity 维度，是最粗粒度的方案。本设计在 Queue 级别提供了更精细的 capacity 维度配额管理。 --- # 遗留问题与后续工作
问题 当前处理 后续计划
DeviceClass 名与 Extended Resource 命名冲突 `deviceclass/` 前缀隔离，无冲突 无
DRAPartitionableDevices（MIG 分区） 暂不支持 后续 KEP 跟进
Topology Aware Scheduling + DRA 暂不支持 独立 KEP
`allocationMode: All` worst-case 估算 ResourceSlice 中查最大值 可优化为运行时动态感知
QueueStatus 的 DRA 已分配量展示 需扩展 `QueueStatus.DRAAllocated` Phase 2
--- # 参考链接 - [Kubernetes DRA 文档](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/dynamic-resource-allocation/) - [KEP-5075: DRA Consumable Capacity](https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/5075-dra-consumable-capacity) - [Kueue KEP-2941: DRA Support](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/tree/main/keps/2941-DRA) - [HAMi k8s-dra-driver](https://github.com/Project-HAMi/k8s-dra-driver) - [K8s ResourceQuota for DRA](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/resource-quotas/#quota-for-dra-resource-claims) - [Volcano Capacity Plugin 设计](https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano/blob/master/docs/design/capacity-scheduling.md) "}

## Go 实现：Key 解析 ```go // resource_key.go const ( DeviceClassCountPrefix = "deviceclass/" DeviceClassCapacitySep = ".deviceclass/" ) type ResourceKeyType int const ( KeyTypeTraditional ResourceKeyType = iota // cpu, memory, nvidia.com/gpu KeyTypeDeviceClassCount // deviceclass/ KeyTypeDeviceClassCap // .deviceclass/ ) type ParsedResourceKey struct { Type ResourceKeyType DeviceClassName string // 用于 Count 和 Cap CapacityDim string // 仅 Cap 时有效 } func ParseResourceKey(r v1.ResourceName) ParsedResourceKey { s := string(r) // 优先检查 capacity 格式（含 ".deviceclass/"） if idx := strings.Index(s, DeviceClassCapacitySep); idx > 0 { return ParsedResourceKey{ Type: KeyTypeDeviceClassCap, DeviceClassName: s[idx+len(DeviceClassCapacitySep):], CapacityDim: s[:idx], } } // 再检查 count 格式（以 "deviceclass/" 开头） if strings.HasPrefix(s, DeviceClassCountPrefix) { return ParsedResourceKey{ Type: KeyTypeDeviceClassCount, DeviceClassName: strings.TrimPrefix(s, DeviceClassCountPrefix), } } return ParsedResourceKey{Type: KeyTypeTraditional} } // 构造工具函数（供 controller 和测试使用） func DeviceClassCountKey(dcName string) v1.ResourceName { return v1.ResourceName(DeviceClassCountPrefix + dcName) } func DeviceClassCapKey(dcName, dim string) v1.ResourceName { return v1.ResourceName(dim + DeviceClassCapacitySep + dcName) } ``` ## Go 实现：DRA 请求提取 ```go // 从 Pod 的 ResourceClaim 中提取 DRA 资源请求，翻译为统一 ResourceList func (cp *capacityPlugin) calcTaskDRAResources(task *api.TaskInfo) v1.ResourceList { result := v1.ResourceList{} for _, claimRef := range task.Pod.Spec.ResourceClaims { var tmpl *resourcev1.ResourceClaimTemplate if claimRef.ResourceClaimTemplateName != nil { // ResourceClaimTemplate 模式（主流） key := task.Namespace + "/" + *claimRef.ResourceClaimTemplateName tmpl = cp.claimTemplateCache[key] } else if claimRef.ResourceClaimName != nil { // 直接 ResourceClaim 引用（从 claim 读取 spec） // ... } if tmpl == nil { continue } for _, req := range tmpl.Spec.Spec.Devices.Requests { dcName := req.Exactly.DeviceClassName count := int64(1) if req.Exactly.Count != nil { count = *req.Exactly.Count } // 设备数量 countKey := DeviceClassCountKey(dcName) result[countKey] = addQuantity(result[countKey], count) // consumable capacity 维度 for dim, qty := range req.Exactly.Capacity.Requests { capKey := DeviceClassCapKey(dcName, string(dim)) result[capKey] = addQuantity(result[capKey], qty) } } } return result } ``` ## Capacity Plugin 集成（改动最小化） ```go // capacity plugin 核心改动仅在「请求量合并」阶段 // checkQuota 逻辑本身不变，因为 ResourceList 已统一 func (cp *capacityPlugin) Allocatable(queue *api.QueueInfo, task *api.TaskInfo) bool { // 合并传统资源请求 + DRA 资源请求 requested := task.Resreq.Clone() draResources := cp.calcTaskDRAResources(task) for k, v := range draResources { requested[k] = v } // 现有的 LessEqualWithDimension 逻辑直接复用 // capability 中现在同时包含传统 key 和 deviceclass/ key return requested.LessEqualWithDimension(queue.Queue.Spec.Capability, task) } ``` ## ResourceClaim 状态生命周期追踪 ```javascript ResourceClaim 状态 Volcano capacity plugin 动作 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Pending（未分配） 不计入 draAllocated Allocated + Reserved（绑定到 Pod） 计入 draAllocated Released（Pod 完成/删除） 从 draAllocated 移除 ``` 实现方式：Watch `ResourceClaim` 对象，在 `status.allocation` 字段变化时更新 Queue 的 DRA 已分配量。共享 ResourceClaim（多 Pod 引用同一 claim）按 **ResourceClaim 为单位计数一次**，与 K8s 原生 ResourceQuota 行为一致。 ## allocationMode: All 的处理（是否要支持） 当 ResourceClaim 使用 `allocationMode: All` 时（分配该 DeviceClass 下所有设备），在 ResourceSlice 中查询该 DeviceClass 对应的最大设备数（可能会导致计算额度超过队列quota配置），并计入 quota。 --- # 完整配置示例 ## 场景一：整卡 GPU 队列 ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: gpu-team spec: capability: cpu: "64" memory: "256Gi" # DRA 整卡，最多 8 块 "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8" deserved: "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "4" guarantee: "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "1" ``` ## 场景二：vGPU 队列（HAMi consumable capacity） ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: vgpu-team spec: capability: cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # 不限制消耗几块物理 GPU，仅限制总 capacity "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "800" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "320Gi" deserved: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "400" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "160Gi" guarantee: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "100" ``` ## 场景三：混合队列（整卡 + vGPU + 传统 Device Plugin） ```yaml apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1 kind: Queue metadata: name: ml-team spec: capability: # 传统资源 cpu: "100" memory: "200Gi" # Device Plugin GPU（老方式，继续支持） "nvidia.com/gpu": "4" # DRA 整卡 "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8" # DRA vGPU（同时限制数量上限和 capacity 总量） "deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "20" "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "800" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "320Gi" deserved: cpu: "60" memory: "120Gi" "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "4" "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "400" guarantee: "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "100" "memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io": "40Gi" ``` --- # 与参考方案的横向对比 ## vs. Kueue KEP-2941
维度 Kueue 方案 Volcano 方案（本设计）
DeviceClass 映射 全局 ConfigMap `DeviceClassMappings`，DeviceClass → 逻辑名 直接用 `deviceclass/` key，无需额外配置层
consumable capacity ❌ 不支持，仅 count ✅ `.deviceclass/` 支持
ResourceClaim 支持 仅 Template，不支持直接 Claim 两者均支持
与现有 quota 集成 ResourceFlavor + ClusterQueue，结构独立 统一进 `capability` ResourceList，复用现有逻辑
弹性调度（deserved） BorrowingLimit / LendingLimit 与现有 `deserved`/`guarantee` 统一
## vs. K8s 原生 ResourceQuota K8s 原生 ResourceQuota 仅支持 `count/resourceclaims`（按对象数量统计），无法感知 capacity 维度，是最粗粒度的方案。本设计在 Queue 级别提供了更精细的 capacity 维度配额管理。 --- # 遗留问题与后续工作
问题 当前处理 后续计划
DeviceClass 名与 Extended Resource 命名冲突 `deviceclass/` 前缀隔离，无冲突 无
DRAPartitionableDevices（MIG 分区） 暂不支持 后续 KEP 跟进
Topology Aware Scheduling + DRA 暂不支持 独立 KEP
`allocationMode: All` worst-case 估算 ResourceSlice 中查最大值 可优化为运行时动态感知
QueueStatus 的 DRA 已分配量展示 需扩展 `QueueStatus.DRAAllocated` Phase 2
--- # 参考链接 - [Kubernetes DRA 文档](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/dynamic-resource-allocation/) - [KEP-5075: DRA Consumable Capacity](https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/5075-dra-consumable-capacity) - [Kueue KEP-2941: DRA Support](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/tree/main/keps/2941-DRA) - [HAMi k8s-dra-driver](https://github.com/Project-HAMi/k8s-dra-driver) - [K8s ResourceQuota for DRA](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/resource-quotas/#quota-for-dra-resource-claims) - [Volcano Capacity Plugin 设计](https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano/blob/master/docs/design/capacity-scheduling.md) "}

我已经读完了 Notion 文档。两边的设计方案存在**根本性差异**，核心在于 DRA quota 的 API 表达方式不同。

---

## 方案对比

| 维度 | 当前 repo 文档（方案 A） | Notion 文档（方案 B，推荐） |
|------|------------------------|--------------------------|
| **API 结构** | `spec.dra.capability` 独立字段，嵌套 `DRAQuota` 结构体 | 直接复用 `spec.capability` ResourceList，用 `deviceclass/` 前缀 |
| **配置统一性** | 传统资源和 DRA 分开配置 | 统一在同一个 ResourceList 里 |
| **弹性抢占** | `deserved`/`guarantee` 需单独实现 | 直接复用 capacity plugin 现有逻辑 |
| **层级队列** | 需要全新代码路径 | 自动继承现有实现 |
| **改动量** | 大（新类型、新逻辑） | 小（主要是 key 解析 + 请求提取） |

## Notion 方案 B 的核心思路

通过 key 前缀约定将 DRA 资源"扁平化"到现有 ResourceList 中：

- **整卡模式**：`deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com: "8"` — 设备数量限制
- **consumable capacity**：`cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: "800"` — capacity 维度限制
- **传统资源**：`cpu`、`memory`、`nvidia.com/gpu` 等照旧不变

解析规则只有三条：含 `.deviceclass/` → capacity 维度；以 `deviceclass/` 开头 → 设备数量；其他 → 传统路径。

## 关键优势

方案 B 最大的优势在于**复用**。因为 DRA quota 表达为普通的 ResourceList key-value，capacity plugin 现有的：
- `LessEqualWithDimension` quota 检查
- `deserved`/`guarantee` 弹性抢占
- 父子队列继承

都**不需要改动**，只需要在"请求量合并"阶段把 Pod 的 ResourceClaim 翻译成对应的 `deviceclass/` key 加入 ResourceList 即可。

## 当前 repo 文档的问题

对照 Notion 文档，repo 中的两份文档存在以下需要修正的地方：

1. **`capacity-dra-support.md`**：整体按方案 A 设计，需要重写为方案 B（`deviceclass/` 前缀方案）
2. **`how_to_use_dra_quota.md`**：所有 YAML 示例使用了 `spec.dra.capability` 结构，需要改为 `deviceclass/` key 风格
3. **Go API 定义**：不再需要 `DRAQuota`、`DRAResourceQuota` 类型，改为 key 解析函数
4. 用户指南中 `deserved`/`guarantee` 描述为"当前不支持"，但方案 B 下它们天然可用

---

要我按照 Notion 文档的方案 B 重写这两份 repo 文档吗？还是你想先讨论方案选择或具体的实现细节？

content.content:
# Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) - Technical Details

## Overview
DRA is a Kubernetes feature (stable in v1.35) that enables flexible allocation of devices and resources to Pods, similar to dynamic volume provisioning. It allows device drivers and cluster admins to define device classes that workload operators can claim in their workloads.

## Core API Resources

All DRA resources belong to the `resource.k8s.io/v1` API group.

### 1. DeviceClass

Defines categories of devices available in the cluster and how to select specific device attributes.

**Purpose:**
- Created by device drivers or cluster admins
- Provides operators with hardware categories optimized for use cases
- Uses CEL (Common Expression Language) for fine-grained filtering

**Key Characteristics:**
- DeviceClass parameters can match zero or more devices in ResourceSlices
- ResourceClaims reference DeviceClasses and select specific device attributes
- Supports flexible device filtering based on custom attributes

### 2. ResourceClaim

Describes a request for access to attached resources (devices) in the cluster.

**Structure:**
```yaml
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: example-claim
  namespace: default
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
      - deviceClassName: "accelerator"
        selectors:
          - matchExpressions:
              - name: "memory"
                operator: "gt"
                values: ["4Gi"]
    allocationMode: "ExactCount"  # or "All"
    count: 1
  shareable: true  # Allow sharing across pods
```

**Key Fields:**

| Field | Purpose | Details |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `devices.requests` | Device requests | References DeviceClass and selects devices |
| `selectors` | Filter devices | Uses CEL expressions for fine-grained filtering |
| `allocationMode` | Allocation strategy | `ExactCount` or `All` |
| `count` | Exact count mode | Number of devices to allocate (when using ExactCount) |
| `shareable` | Sharing capability | Allow multiple Pods to share the same claim |
| `adminAccess` | Beta feature | Provides admin-level access to devices |

**Request Structure Details:**
- **Exactly vs Selectors**: 
  - `selectors`: Use CEL expressions for attribute-based filtering (preferred approach)
  - Available parameters depend on device type and drivers

**Allocation Modes:**
- `ExactCount`: Allocate exactly N devices as specified in `count`
- `All`: Allocate all matching devices

### 3. ResourceClaimTemplate

Template used by Kubernetes to auto-generate per-Pod ResourceClaims.

**Use Cases:**
- Each Pod gets independent access to separate, similarly-configured devices
- Kubernetes generates ResourceClaims from the template specification
- Lifetime of each generated ResourceClaim is bound to the corresponding Pod
- When Pod terminates, the ResourceClaim is automatically deleted

**When to Use:**
- Use `ResourceClaimTemplate` when Pods need independent access
- Use direct `ResourceClaim` when multiple Pods share access to specific devices

**Key Difference from Direct ResourceClaim:**
```yaml
# ResourceClaimTemplate approach - auto-generated per Pod
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      devices:
        requests:
          - deviceClassName: "gpu"

# Direct ResourceClaim approach - shared across Pods
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
      - deviceClassName: "gpu"
  shareable: true
```

### 4. ResourceSlice

Represents one or more resources attached to nodes (e.g., devices).

**Purpose:**
- Created and managed by device drivers
- Provides Kubernetes with information about:
  - Available devices on nodes
  - Device capacity and attributes
  - Resource availability changes
- Used by Kubernetes scheduler to find suitable nodes for ResourceClaims

**Device Publishing Model:**
- Drivers create/update ResourceSlices when capacity changes
- Kubernetes uses ResourceSlices during scheduling to match Claims with available devices
- ResourceSlices contain node affinity information (which nodes have the devices)

## Pod Integration

### Referencing ResourceClaims in Pods

**Direct ResourceClaim Reference:**
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: example-pod
spec:
  resourceClaims:
    - name: my-claim
      source:
        resourceClaimName: "existing-claim"  # Must exist in same namespace
  containers:
    - name: app
      image: myapp:latest
      resources:
        claims:
          - name: my-claim
```

**ResourceClaimTemplate Reference:**
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: example-deployment
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      resourceClaims:
        - name: gpu-claim
          source:
            resourceClaimTemplateName: "gpu-template"
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: myapp:latest
          resources:
            claims:
              - name: gpu-claim
```

**Requirements:**
- Direct ResourceClaim: Must exist in the same namespace as the Pod
- If ResourceClaim doesn't exist, Pod won't schedule
- Behavior similar to PersistentVolumeClaim namespace requirements
- ResourceClaimTemplate: Kubernetes generates claims automatically

## Advanced Features

### 1. Prioritized List (Beta - v1.34+)

Allows specifying multiple device requests with priorities. Kubernetes evaluates requests in order and allocates based on availability and priority.

### 2. AdminAccess (Beta)

Special access mode that provides admin-level device access to Pods (specific implementation depends on device driver).

### 3. Device Sharing

**Shareable Claims:**
```yaml
spec:
  shareable: true  # Multiple Pods can reference this claim
```

Benefits:
- Multiple containers or Pods can share the same device
- Reduces device consumption for compatible workloads
- Cluster admin can manage shared device allocation

### 4. Device Taints and Tolerations (Consumable Capacity)

**Device Taints:**
- Devices can be tainted to restrict allocation
- Similar to Kubernetes node taints
- Pod tolerations must match device taints for scheduling

**Capacity Dimensions:**
- Devices may support partial allocation (partitionable devices)
- Capacity can be expressed in consumable units
- Device health monitoring tracks device availability

### 5. Device Health Monitoring

**ResourceClaim Device Status:**
- Tracks health status of allocated devices
- Kubelet reports device metrics
- Provides observability of device allocation and health

**Kubelet Device Metrics:**
- Device utilization metrics
- Device health status
- Available through Kubernetes metrics API

## Workflow Summary

### User Workflow
1. **Device Owner/Cluster Admin**: Create `DeviceClass` and install DRA drivers
2. **Cluster Admin**: Attach devices to nodes, ensure ResourceSlices are published
3. **Workload Operator**: 
   - Create `ResourceClaim` or `ResourceClaimTemplate`
   - Reference in Pod/Deployment `spec.resourceClaims`
   - Container references claim in `resources.claims`

### Kubernetes Workflow
1. Pod creation requests ResourceClaim
2. Kubernetes queries ResourceSlices for available devices
3. Scheduler matches ResourceClaim requests against available devices
4. Devices are allocated to the ResourceClaim
5. Pod is scheduled on node with access to allocated devices
6. Device driver configures device access for containers

## Key Differences from Device Plugins

| Aspect | Device Plugins | DRA |
|--------|---|---|
| Device Filtering | Simple quantity requests | CEL-based fine-grained filtering |
| Device Sharing | Not supported | Supported (shareable claims) |
| Device Categorization | Per-device | Device classes with centralized categorization |
| Pod Request Specification | Per-container device limits | Single claim reference per Pod |
| Flexibility | Limited | High (selectors, constraints, priorities) |

## Setup and Configuration

See [Set Up DRA in a Cluster](/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/assign-resources/set-up-dra-cluster/) for cluster configuration.

See [Allocate Devices to Workloads with DRA](/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/assign-resources/allocate-devices-dra/) for workload allocation examples.

- Provides observability of device allocation and health

**Kubelet Device Metrics:**
- Device utilization metrics
- Device health status
- Available through Kubernetes metrics API

## Workflow Summary

### User Workflow
1. **Device Owner/Cluster Admin**: Create `DeviceClass` and install DRA drivers
2. **Cluster Admin**: Attach devices to nodes, ensure ResourceSlices are published
3. **Workload Operator**: 
   - Create `ResourceClaim` or `ResourceClaimTemplate`
   - Reference in Pod/Deployment `spec.resourceClaims`
   - Container references claim in `resources.claims`

### Kubernetes Workflow
1. Pod creation requests ResourceClaim
2. Kubernetes queries ResourceSlices for available devices
3. Scheduler matches ResourceClaim requests against available devices
4. Devices are allocated to the ResourceClaim
5. Pod is scheduled on node with access to allocated devices
6. Device driver configures device access for containers

## Key Differences from Device Plugins

| Aspect | Device Plugins | DRA |
|--------|---|---|
| Device Filtering | Simple quantity requests | CEL-based fine-grained filtering |
| Device Sharing | Not supported | Supported (shareable claims) |
| Device Categorization | Per-device | Device classes with centralized categorization |
| Pod Request Specification | Per-container device limits | Single claim reference per Pod |
| Flexibility | Limited | High (selectors, constraints, priorities) |

## Setup and Configuration

See [Set Up DRA in a Cluster](/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/assign-resources/set-up-dra-cluster/) for cluster configuration.

See [Allocate Devices to Workloads with DRA](/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/assign-resources/allocate-devices-dra/) for workload allocation examples.

## 深挖的技术问题

### 1. K8s DRA v1 API 结构与 Notion 代码不匹配

Notion 文档中引用了 `req.Exactly.DeviceClassName`、`req.Exactly.Count`、`req.Exactly.Capacity.Requests`，但根据 K8s DRA v1（1.35 stable）API，ResourceClaim 的 request 结构是：

```go
// K8s resource.k8s.io/v1
type DeviceRequest struct {
    Name            string
    DeviceClassName string        // 直接在 request 层级
    Selectors       []DeviceSelector  // CEL 表达式
    AllocationMode  DeviceAllocationMode  // ExactCount | All
    Count           int64
    // AdminAccess  *bool  (beta)
}
```

**没有 `Exactly` 这个子结构**。`deviceClassName` 和 `count` 直接在 request 上。需要确认：你们实际对接的 K8s 版本和 DRA API 版本是哪个？如果是 KEP-5075 consumable capacity（还在 alpha），`Capacity.Requests` 的 API 位置可能也不同。

### 2. Shared ResourceClaim 的 quota 重复计算

两份设计都没有充分讨论 **shareable ResourceClaim** 的场景：

```yaml
# 一个 claim，shareable: true
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: shared-gpu
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - deviceClassName: gpu.nvidia.com
      count: 2
  shareable: true
```

如果 queue-a 中有 3 个 Pod 都引用 `shared-gpu`，quota 应该计算几次？
- **按 claim 计一次**：物理上确实只占用 2 个 GPU，但需要 claim 级别去重逻辑
- **按 pod 计多次**：会过度消耗 quota，但实现简单

当前 cache 代码 `buildTaskDRAResreq()` 是 per-task 提取的，看起来会重复计算。这对 quota 公平性有影响。

### 3. allocationMode: All 的 quota 困境

Notion 文档提到"从 ResourceSlice 中查询最大值"，但这有几个问题：

- **调度时 ResourceSlice 状态会变化**：设备可能在线/离线，count 不确定
- **All 模式的语义是"给我这个 class 下所有设备"**：这可能跨多个节点
- **quota 该记多少？** 如果集群有 32 块 H100，一个 `All` 请求就消耗 32 的 quota？如果队列 capability 只有 8 呢——是直接拒绝还是按实际分配数计？

这个问题在 Kueue KEP-2941 中也没有很好解决。我建议 MVP 阶段直接 **不支持 allocationMode: All 的 quota 追踪**，遇到 All 模式 skip 或 warn。

### 4. 方案 B 与现有 `api.Resource` 的兼容性

当前 repo 的 `api.Resource` 结构：

```go
type Resource struct {
    MilliCPU float64
    Memory   float64
    ScalarResources map[v1.ResourceName]float64
}
```

方案 B 要把 `deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com: "8"` 放进 queue 的 `capability` ResourceList。这意味着 `deviceclass/` 前缀的 key 会进入 `ScalarResources` map。这在**比较逻辑**上可以工作（`LessEqual` 遍历 map 逐 key 比较），但有几个隐患：

- **`share` 计算**：`share = max(allocated[rn] / deserved[rn])`，DRA 维度会参与 share 计算，可能影响传统资源的调度优先级。一个 GPU 队列和一个纯 CPU 队列的 share 比较是否合理？
- **`totalResource` 汇总**：capacity plugin 汇总集群总资源来计算 deserved 分配。DRA 资源的集群总量从哪来？传统资源从 node 汇总，但 DRA 设备信息在 ResourceSlice 中，不在 Node.Status.Allocatable 里。
- **`Intersection` / `Diff` 等工具函数**：这些函数作用于 Resource 的所有 key，DRA key 混入后，逻辑是否正确？

### 5. 从 Pod 提取 DRA 请求的完整路径

这是两个方案都需要解决的核心问题，但细节不够：

```
Pod.Spec.ResourceClaims[] 
  → 引用方式：ResourceClaimName / ResourceClaimTemplateName
  → 需要查找实际的 Claim 或 Template 对象
  → 提取 Devices.Requests[]
  → 每个 request 有 DeviceClassName + Count + (Capacity?)
  → 聚合为 quota 消耗
```

问题链：
1. **ResourceClaimTemplate 场景**：Template 在 Pod 创建时生成 ResourceClaim，但在 Volcano 调度阶段，生成的 claim 可能还不存在（Volcano 的 PodGroup 可能先于 Pod 创建）
2. **Pod 层级引用 vs Container 层级引用**：`pod.spec.resourceClaims` 定义 claim 引用，`container.resources.claims` 指定哪个 container 用哪个 claim。quota 应该以 pod 层级为准
3. **当前 cache 实现**用了 `resourceClaimCache`（assumecache），如果 claim 还未创建怎么处理？

### 6. Reclaim/Preemption 对 DRA 资源的语义

方案 B 的最大卖点是复用 `deserved`/`guarantee` 弹性抢占。但 DRA 资源的 preemption 有本质区别：

- **传统资源**：evict 一个 Pod，CPU/Memory 立即释放
- **DRA 资源**：evict Pod 后，ResourceClaim 可能还存在（特别是非 template 模式的 claim），设备不会立即释放。需要等 claim 被删除或 released

这意味着 **reclaim 一个 DRA 任务后，quota 不会立即回收**。capacity plugin 的 reclaim 逻辑假设 evict = 立即释放，对 DRA 可能不成立。

### 7. 层级队列中 DRA 资源的 deserved 分配算法

capacity plugin 的 deserved 分配依赖集群总资源（`totalResource`）。对于传统资源，这来自 node status。对于 DRA：

- `totalResource` 中 `deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com` 的值从哪来？
- 是否需要 watch 所有 ResourceSlice 并汇总设备总量？
- 如果不汇总，hierarchical queue 的 deserved 按比例分配就缺少分母

---

| 优先级 | 问题 | 影响 |
|--------|------|------|
| **P0** | K8s DRA API 版本确认（`Exactly` vs 直接字段） | 代码能否编译 |
| **P0** | 方案 A→B 是否重构（已有完整方案 A 实现） | 工作量估算 |
| **P1** | Shared claim quota 去重 | quota 准确性 |
| **P1** | totalResource 中 DRA 设备总量来源 | deserved 分配正确性 |
| **P1** | Reclaim 后 DRA 释放延迟 | 弹性调度正确性 |
| **P2** | allocationMode: All 处理策略 | 边缘场景 |
| **P2** | share 计算中 DRA 维度影响 | 调度公平性 |

## 问题 1：K8s DRA API 结构确认

**结论：当前代码的 `req.Exactly` 路径是正确的。** K8s v0.35.0 的 `DeviceRequest` 结构：

```go
type DeviceRequest struct {
    Name           string
    Exactly        *ExactDeviceRequest      // 精确请求
    FirstAvailable []DeviceSubRequest       // 优先级列表（beta, v1.34+）
}

type ExactDeviceRequest struct {
    DeviceClassName string
    Selectors       []DeviceSelector
    AllocationMode  DeviceAllocationMode   // ExactCount | All
    Count           int64
    AdminAccess     *bool
    Tolerations     []DeviceToleration
    Capacity        *CapacityRequirements  // KEP-5075 consumable capacity
}

type CapacityRequirements struct {
    Requests map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity
}
```

Notion 文档中的 `req.Exactly.DeviceClassName`、`req.Exactly.Count`、`req.Exactly.Capacity.Requests` 路径都是正确的。

**但发现了一个新遗漏：`FirstAvailable` 未处理。** 这是 K8s 1.34+ 的 prioritized list 特性——用户可以提供多个备选请求，scheduler 选第一个能满足的。当前代码（`cache.go:1796`）只处理了 `req.Exactly != nil` 的情况，完全跳过了 `FirstAvailable`。

对 `FirstAvailable` 我的看法是：

- 对于 **quota 预估**，应该取 `FirstAvailable` 中的 **worst case**（最大资源消耗的子请求）作为 quota 预留，因为调度时你不知道哪个会被选中
- 或者 MVP 阶段先不支持，遇到 `FirstAvailable` 时 skip 并 log warning

## 问题 3：Shared ResourceClaim 的 quota 去重

场景：一个 `shareable: true` 的 ResourceClaim 被同一队列中多个 Pod 引用。

```yaml
# 一个 claim，请求 2 块 GPU
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: shared-gpu
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: req1
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: gpu.nvidia.com
        count: 2
  shareable: true
---
# Pod A 和 Pod B 都引用 shared-gpu
```

物理上只消耗 2 块 GPU，但如果 per-task 提取 DRA 请求，会被计算 2 次（4 块 GPU），导致 quota 虚高。

两种处理方式：

| 方式 | 优点 | 缺点 |
|------|------|------|
| **按 claim 去重**：同一 ResourceClaim 在同一队列中只计一次 quota | quota 准确反映物理消耗 | 需要维护 claim→queue 的已计数集合，实现复杂 |
| **按 pod 重复计**：每个 pod 引用都计一次 | 实现简单，与现有 per-task 逻辑一致 | quota 偏保守，可能导致误拒 |

我倾向于 MVP 阶段**按 pod 重复计**，理由：
1. shared claim 在实际使用中不常见（大多数是 template 模式，每个 pod 独立 claim）
2. 偏保守的 quota 不会导致资源超分，只是可能提前拒绝
3. 去重逻辑引入跨 task 的状态追踪，增加复杂度和 bug 风险

content.text:
当前已有 `resourceClaimCache`（AssumeCache），已经缓存了集群所有 ResourceClaim 对象。基础设施是现成的。

核心问题是 **去重的位置和机制**。在方案 B 中，DRA 资源会合并到 task 的 `Resource.ScalarResources`。如果两个 task 引用同一 claim，它们各自的 resource request 都包含该 claim 的资源量。当 capacity plugin 把两个 task 的 allocated 相加时，就重复计算了。

去重方案需要两个要素：

**1. TaskInfo 上记录引用的 claim 列表**

```go
type TaskInfo struct {
    // ... 现有字段 ...
    // DRA claim references (namespace/name → 已提取的资源量)
    DRAClaimKeys []string  // e.g., ["default/shared-gpu"]
}
```

**2. Capacity plugin 在 queue 级别维护已计数的 claim 集合**

```go
type queueAttr struct {
    // ... 现有字段 ...
    // 已计入 allocated 的 claim (key: namespace/claimName)
    draCountedClaims map[string]bool
}
```

当 allocate 一个 task 时：
- 遍历 task 的 `DRAClaimKeys`
- 如果 claim 已在 `draCountedClaims` 中 → 跳过该 claim 的 DRA 资源，不加入 allocated
- 如果是新 claim → 加入 allocated，并记入 `draCountedClaims`

当 deallocate 一个 task 时：
- 检查该 claim 是否还有其他 task 在本队列中引用
- 如果没有了 → 从 allocated 减去，并从 `draCountedClaims` 移除

deallocate 时的"是否还有其他 task 引用"检查需要**引用计数**，所以更准确的结构应该是：

```go
// key: namespace/claimName, value: 引用该 claim 的 task 数量
draClaimRefCount map[string]int
```

这样：
- allocate: `refCount++`，仅当 `refCount` 从 0→1 时加入 allocated
- deallocate: `refCount--`，仅当 `refCount` 从 1→0 时从 allocated 减去

你觉得这个去重机制 OK 吗？或者有更简单的思路？

| `draCountedClaims` | `resourceClaimRefs` | 已调整 |
| `buildTaskDRAResreq` | `buildTaskResourceClaimResources` | 描述实际动作：从 ResourceClaim 提取资源 |
| `calcTaskDRAResources` | `calcTaskResourceClaimResources` | 同上 |
| `checkDRAAllocatable` | 删除，复用现有 quota 检查 | 方案 B 统一路径 |
| `updateDRAAllocated` | 删除，复用现有 allocated 更新 | 同上 |
| `dynamicResourceAllocationEnable` | 保留（这是 K8s feature gate 名） | 这是 K8s 官方术语 |
| `DeviceClassCountPrefix` | `DeviceClassCountPrefix` | 描述的是 DeviceClass 资源，不是 DRA 抽象概念 |
| `DeviceClassCapacitySep` | `DeviceClassCapacitySep` | 同上 |

原则：
- **K8s feature gate 名**（`DynamicResourceAllocation`）保留原样，这是上游术语
- **代码标识符**用具体资源类型命名：`ResourceClaim`、`DeviceClass`、`ResourceSlice`
- **配置 key** 用 `deviceclass/` 前缀，语义直接指向 K8s `DeviceClass` 对象

---

继续下一个问题。

## 问题 4：`totalResource` 中 DRA 设备总量的来源

capacity plugin 的 deserved 分配算法依赖 `totalResource`（集群总资源）来按比例分配。对于传统资源，来源是 `node.Status.Allocatable` 的汇总。

但 DRA 设备信息**不在 Node.Status.Allocatable 中**，而是在 `ResourceSlice` 对象里。要让 `deserved` 对 `deviceclass/` 资源正确分配，需要知道集群中某个 DeviceClass 的总设备数和总 capacity。

三种思路：

| 方式 | 描述 | 优缺点 |
|------|------|--------|
| **A. 从 ResourceSlice 汇总** | watch ResourceSlice，按 DeviceClass 汇总设备总量和 capacity | 准确，但需要新增 ResourceSlice 聚合逻辑，且 ResourceSlice 变化频繁 |
| **B. 从 Queue 配置推导** | `totalResource[deviceclass/X]` = 所有 queue 的 `capability[deviceclass/X]` 之和 | 简单，但不反映物理真实容量，管理员可能 over-commit |
| **C. 不自动计算，要求管理员显式配置 deserved** | 对 `deviceclass/` 资源不参与自动 deserved 分配，必须手动配置 | 最简单，语义明确，但灵活性差 |

我倾向于 **方式 C（MVP）**，理由：
- capacity plugin 现有的自动 deserved 分配是按 `request / totalRequest * totalResource` 比例计算的，对 DRA 来说这个比例意义不大（DRA 设备通常是固定数量的离散资源）
- ResourceSlice 的汇总逻辑复杂度高，且 consumable capacity 场景下同一物理设备可能被多次分配
- 管理员在配置 `deviceclass/` capability 时，同时配置 deserved 和 guarantee 是自然的操作

你怎么看？

好思路，和现有 `totalResource` 的逻辑保持一致：node 资源也是从集群汇总，root queue 可以手动 override。

具体方案：

**1. 从 ResourceSlice 汇总 DeviceClass 总量**

当前 cache 已经有 `ResourceSliceTracker`（`cache.go:817`），可以基于它聚合：

```
遍历所有 ResourceSlice:
  对每个 device:
    deviceClassName → count++
    如果 device 有 capacity 维度 → 累加各维度总量
    
输出:
  deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com: 8
  deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: 20
  cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: 2000
  memory.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io: 640Gi
```

这里有个细节需要确认——consumable capacity 的设备（`allowMultipleAllocations: true`）的总量是取 **capacity 上限**而非设备数量。比如一块物理 GPU 有 100 cores 的 capacity，20 块就是 2000 cores。

**2. root queue 手动 override**

```yaml
# root queue 显式指定 DeviceClass 总量
apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
kind: Queue
metadata:
  name: root
spec:
  capability:
    cpu: "500"
    memory: "1Ti"
    "deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com": "8"
    "cores.deviceclass/hami-core-gpu.io": "1600"  # 管理员认为可用的只有 1600
```

**3. 优先级与 warning**

```
if root queue 手动配置了 deviceclass/X:
    totalResource[deviceclass/X] = root queue 配置值
    if root 配置值 != ResourceSlice 汇总值:
        log.Warningf("DeviceClass %s: root queue capability (%v) differs from cluster actual (%v)", ...)
else:
    totalResource[deviceclass/X] = ResourceSlice 汇总值
```

这里有两个实现问题想和你确认：

**问题 A：ResourceSlice 汇总的时机**

现有 `totalResource` 是在 `OnSessionOpen` 时从 node snapshot 汇总的。DeviceClass 的汇总也应该在 `OnSessionOpen` 时从 ResourceSliceTracker 做一次 snapshot 汇总，保持一致。不需要实时 watch 变更，每个调度周期开始时重新算就行。

**问题 B：ResourceSlice 中 capacity 维度的提取**

ResourceSlice 的 device 结构中，capacity 维度在 `device.Basic.Capacity` 里（consumable capacity 场景）。需要遍历每个 slice 的每个 device，按 DeviceClass 聚合。这个聚合逻辑放在 cache 层还是 capacity plugin 层？

我倾向于放在 **cache 层**，在 `OnSessionOpen` 时提供一个 `GetDeviceClassTotalResources() v1.ResourceList` 给 plugin 用，和 node 资源汇总的模式对齐。

你觉得这两点 OK 吗？

content.content:
# DRA Partitionable Devices KEP Technical Summary

## Problem Statement & Motivation

The KEP addresses the inability to dynamically partition devices in "structured parameters" DRA. Two primary use cases motivate this work:

**1. NVIDIA MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) Partitioning:**
GPUs can be divided into multiple logical instances consuming different portions of shared resources (memory slices, multiprocessors, specialized engines). Rather than pre-partitioning devices statically, vendors need to advertise overlapping partitions so the scheduler prevents conflicting allocations—enabling dynamic partitioning after scheduling.

**2. Multi-Host TPU Scheduling:**
TPUs connected via high-speed interconnects require topology-aware allocation. A 64-TPU pool supports specific valid slices (8×8, 4×8, 4×4, 2×4 configurations across nodes). Users must request valid topological groupings, not arbitrary TPU counts.

## Core Modeling Concepts

### Counter Sets
The proposal introduces **counter sets**—named collections of shared counters representing finite resources within a physical device. Each counter tracks available capacity across dimensions (e.g., memory slices, processing units).

Key characteristics:
- Defined at ResourceSlice level via `SharedCounters` field
- Maximum 8 counter sets per ResourceSlice
- Maximum 32 counters per set
- Counter names must be DNS labels and unique within sets

### Device Counter Consumption
Devices reference counter sets through `ConsumesCounters` field, specifying how many units they consume from each counter. This enables the scheduler to track allocation conflicts:

"When a device is allocated, the portion of counters it uses will no longer be available for use by other devices."

The scheduler prevents overlapping allocations by ensuring cumulative consumption doesn't exceed available counters.

### Per-Device Node Selection
A new `PerDeviceNodeSelection` boolean flag (mutually exclusive with ResourceSlice-level node selectors) enables multi-host device representation. Each device can specify its own:
- `NodeName` (specific node)
- `NodeSelector` (matching nodes)
- `AllNodes` (cluster-wide availability)

This allows logical devices spanning multiple machines.

## API Types & Structure

```
ResourceSliceSpec:
├── PerDeviceNodeSelection (bool)
├── SharedCounters[] (CounterSet)
│   └── Name (string)
│       └── Counters[] (map Counter)

Device:
├── Name (string)
├── ConsumesCounters[] (DeviceCounterConsumption)
│   └── CounterSet (reference)
│       └── Counters[] (consumed amounts)
├── NodeName (pointer to string)
├── NodeSelector (pointer to corev1.NodeSelector)
└── AllNodes (pointer to bool)
```

Maximum limits:
- 2 counter sets per device
- 32 counters per DeviceCounterConsumption
- 2,048 total counters across all devices per ResourceSlice

## Capacity Calculation & Partitioning

For MIG example with 8 memory slices total:

**Available profiles define overlapping allocations:**
- 1 × 7g.40gb (consumes 8 slices)
- 2 × 3g.20gb (each consumes 4 slices, can coexist)
- 4 × 1g.5gb (each consumes 2 slices, non-overlapping sets)

The counter mechanism models this by tracking: "the allocation of one MIG device may nullify the ability to allocate some subset of other possible MIG devices (but not all of them)."

## Scheduler Behavior

The scheduler implements counter-based validation:

1. **Feasibility check:** When evaluating device allocation, sum all consumed counters across allocated devices
2. **Conflict detection:** Reject allocation if any counter sum exceeds available capacity
3. **Multi-host validation:** Ensure all requested devices can be satisfied on allowed nodes

Critically, "only those partitions explicitly defined by a ResourceSlice will be allocatable by a user."

## Validation & Error Handling

Cross-ResourceSlice references shift validation from admission to runtime:

"By allowing cross-ResourceSlice references, we will not be able to validate that the references can actually be resolved during admission since we are only able to validate a ResourceSlice in isolation."

Runtime validation confirms:
- Unique counter set names within pools
- Referenced counter sets exist
- Referenced counters exist within sets
- Device name uniqueness

## User-Facing Consistency

The KEP preserves existing ResourceClaim syntax. Users continue selecting devices via CEL expressions on attributes/capacity—the counter mechanism remains internal scheduler logic, invisible to end users.

content.text:
好，现在清楚了。让我整理 partitionable devices 对我们的影响。

---

## Partitionable Devices 分析

### 机制概要

Partitionable devices（KEP-4815）是 **alpha 特性**（K8s 1.35），核心是引入了 **SharedCounters** 机制：

- ResourceSlice 中定义 `SharedCounters`：一组命名的共享计数器，代表物理设备的可分割资源（如 MIG 的 memory slices）
- 每个 device 通过 `ConsumesCounters` 声明它消耗多少 counter
- scheduler 保证所有已分配 device 的 counter 消耗总和不超过可用量
- **对用户透明**：用户仍然用 CEL selector 选设备，counter 冲突检测是 scheduler 内部逻辑

### 典型例子：NVIDIA MIG

一块 A100 有 8 个 memory slices，可以划分为重叠的 partition：
- 1 × 7g.40gb（消耗 8 slices）
- 2 × 3g.20gb（各消耗 4 slices）
- 4 × 1g.5gb（各消耗 2 slices）

这些 partition 作为不同的 device 发布在 ResourceSlice 中，通过 SharedCounters 约束互斥。

### 对 quota 统计的影响

**问题核心：同一物理设备发布了多个重叠的 device，如何计算集群总量？**

以 MIG 为例，一块 A100 可能发布 7 个 device（1×7g + 2×3g + 4×1g），但实际物理容量只有 8 slices。如果简单按 device 数量汇总，总量会严重膨胀。

三种统计策略：

| 策略 | 做法 | 准确性 | 复杂度 |
|------|------|--------|--------|
| **按 device count 汇总** | 所有 device 加起来 | 严重膨胀（7 vs 实际 1 块 GPU） | 低 |
| **按 SharedCounters 汇总** | 取 counter set 的总量作为物理容量 | 准确反映物理资源 | 中 |
| **暂不支持** | 检测到 SharedCounters 时 skip，不计入 totalResource | 安全但功能缺失 | 低 |

我的建议是分阶段：

- **MVP**：检测到 partitionable devices（有 `SharedCounters` 或 `ConsumesCounters`）时 **跳过自动汇总**，log warning。管理员可以通过 root queue 手动指定这类资源的总量。这和我们刚才敲定的"root queue override"机制配合——partitionable devices 场景下管理员必须手动配置。
- **后续**：如果要自动汇总，按 SharedCounters 的 counter 总量计算才是准确的，但这需要理解 counter set 的语义，复杂度较高。

理由：
1. Partitionable devices 本身是 alpha 特性，生产使用有限
2. counter 语义和 DeviceClass quota 不是直接对应关系（counter 是内部调度概念，用户按 DeviceClass 配额）
3. root queue 手动配置对 MIG 场景足够——管理员知道自己有几块 A100、总共多少 slices

你觉得 MVP 先跳过自动汇总 + root queue 手动配置的方案可以吗？

content.text:
好，敲定：

> **Partitionable devices（SharedCounters）MVP 阶段跳过自动汇总**，检测到时 log warning。管理员通过 root queue 手动指定总量。后续再考虑基于 counter set 的自动汇总。

---

## 问题 5：Reclaim/Preemption 后 DRA 资源的释放延迟

传统资源的 reclaim 流程：evict Pod → CPU/Memory 立即从 allocated 中扣减。但 DRA 资源不同：

**ResourceClaim 的生命周期：**

```
Pod 被 evict
  → Pod 删除
    → 如果是 Template 模式：claim 随 pod 自动删除 → 设备释放
    → 如果是直接引用模式：claim 仍然存在（可能被其他 pod 重用）→ 设备不释放
```

这带来一个问题：capacity plugin reclaim 一个 task 后，调用 deallocate 从 `allocated` 扣减资源。但对于直接引用的 claim，设备物理上并没有释放，claim 对象还在，如果马上把 quota 给其他 task 用，可能导致**实际超分**。

不过仔细想一下，我认为**这在 quota 层面其实不是问题**：

1. **Quota 管理的是队列配额，不是物理设备分配**。物理设备的分配由 K8s DRA scheduler 负责，它会检查 ResourceSlice 中设备的实际可用性
2. **Volcano capacity plugin 的 allocated 追踪的是"队列已调度的资源量"**，不是物理设备绑定。当 task 被 evict，这个 task 确实不再占用队列配额了
3. **直接引用的 claim 如果还存在**，它会被另一个 pod 重新引用时再次计入 quota（通过我们的 resourceClaimRefs 机制）
4. 真正的设备分配冲突由 K8s scheduler 的 DRA 插件兜底

所以我的结论是：**reclaim 时按现有逻辑直接扣减 quota 即可，不需要等 claim 物理释放**。Volcano 管 quota 配额，K8s 管物理设备分配，职责分离。

你同意这个判断吗？还是觉得需要额外的保护机制？

---

## 问题 6：allocationMode: All 的处理策略

当 ResourceClaim 的请求使用 `allocationMode: All` 时，语义是"分配该 DeviceClass 下所有匹配设备"。问题在于 **quota 该记多少？** 调度前不知道具体会分配几个设备。

几种策略：

| 策略 | 做法 | 优缺点 |
|------|------|--------|
| **A. 查 ResourceSlice 取最大值** | 从 ResourceSlice 中查该 DeviceClass 的设备总数，作为 quota 消耗 | 准确但偏大（可能跨节点），且依赖 ResourceSlice 汇总 |
| **B. 取 queue capability 作为上限** | 该 DeviceClass 在队列的 capability 就是最大可能消耗 | 过于保守，一个 All 请求就占满队列 |
| **C. 跳过 quota 检查** | 遇到 All 模式不计入 quota，log warning | 简单，但存在 quota 逃逸风险 |
| **D. 不支持，拒绝调度** | 检测到 All 模式时直接 reject | 最安全但限制用户使用 |

结合前面的讨论风格，我倾向于 **C（跳过 + warning）**，理由：

1. `allocationMode: All` 在生产中使用场景有限（主要是测试或独占整机场景）
2. 和 `FirstAvailable` 一样属于"动态请求"，quota 预估不准确
3. 如果管理员真的关心 All 模式的 quota 控制，可以通过限制 DeviceClass 的设备数来间接控制

你怎么看？

敲定：

> **`allocationMode: All` 跳过 quota 检查，log warning。** 与 `FirstAvailable` 处理策略一致。

---

## 问题 7：share 计算中 DRA 维度的影响

capacity plugin 的调度优先级通过 `share` 值决定：

```go
share = max(allocated[rn] / deserved[rn])  // 遍历所有资源维度，取最大比值
```

方案 B 中 `deviceclass/` key 混入 ScalarResources 后，DRA 维度会参与 share 计算。这可能导致：

**场景：**
- queue-A：纯 CPU 任务，deserved cpu=100，已用 80 → share = 0.8
- queue-B：GPU 任务，deserved cpu=50 + `deviceclass/gpu=8`，已用 cpu=20 + gpu=7 → share = max(0.4, 0.875) = 0.875

queue-B 的 share 被 GPU 维度拉高，导致它的 CPU 任务调度优先级也被压低，即使 CPU 使用率只有 40%。

**问题：跨资源类型的 share 比较是否合理？**

其实这不是 DRA 引入的新问题——现有的 `nvidia.com/gpu` 作为 ScalarResource 已经参与 share 计算了。所以 **行为是一致的，不需要特殊处理**。

如果用户觉得某个维度不该影响 share，那是 capacity plugin share 算法本身的优化问题，和 DRA 无关。

你同意这个判断吗？还是希望 DRA 资源在 share 计算中做特殊处理？

好，敲定：

> **DRA 资源维度正常参与 share 计算，不做特殊处理。** 行为与现有 ScalarResource 一致。

---

到这里，我们之前列出的 7 个问题都已讨论完。让我汇总所有决策，同时看看是否还有遗漏。

## 全部决策汇总

| # | 问题 | 决策 |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | K8s DRA API 结构 | `req.Exactly` 路径正确；`FirstAvailable` 跳过 quota 检查，log warning |
| 2 | 方案 A→B | 切到方案 B（`deviceclass/` 前缀），本次主要工作 |
| 3 | Shared ResourceClaim | 按 claim 去重，`resourceClaimRefs map[claimKey]sets.Set[TaskID]` 引用计数 |
| 4 | totalResource 中 DRA 总量 | 从 ResourceSlice 汇总（跳过 partitionable devices）；root queue 手动 override 优先，不一致时 warning |
| 5 | Reclaim 释放延迟 | 直接扣减 quota，不等 claim 物理释放；Volcano 管 quota，K8s 管设备 |
| 6 | allocationMode: All | 跳过 quota 检查，log warning |
| 7 | share 计算 | DRA 维度正常参与，不特殊处理 |
| 命名 | 代码标识符 | 避免 `DRA` 前缀，用 `ResourceClaim`、`DeviceClass` 等实际资源语义命名 |

## 可能遗漏的问题

还有几个点我们没有展开讨论：

**8. ResourceClaimTemplate 的处理**

当前 cache 代码（`cache.go:1780`）只处理 `podClaim.ResourceClaimName != nil` 的情况。但 Template 模式下，pod 引用的是 `ResourceClaimTemplateName`，K8s 会自动生成 claim。生成的 claim 名字是 `<pod-name>-<claim-name>-<random>`。

在 Volcano 调度阶段，这个自动生成的 claim 可能还不存在（PodGroup 先于 Pod 创建）。需要确认：Volcano 是直接读 template spec 来提取资源请求，还是等 claim 生成后再读？

**9. QueueStatus 中 DeviceClass 已分配量的展示**

Notion 文档标注为 Phase 2，但方案 B 下 allocated 本身就在 `Resource.ScalarResources` 里，`deviceclass/` key 可以直接暴露到 QueueStatus 中，不需要额外类型。是否在 MVP 就做？

**10. Webhook 校验**

Queue 的 `deviceclass/` key 是否需要 admission webhook 校验？比如验证 `deviceclass/<name>` 中的 `<name>` 对应集群中真实存在的 DeviceClass？

要继续逐个讨论这三个问题吗？

## 问题 8：ResourceClaimTemplate 的处理

当前 cache 代码只处理了直接引用 claim 的情况：

```go
// cache.go:1779-1783
for _, podClaim := range pod.Spec.ResourceClaims {
    if podClaim.ResourceClaimName == nil {
        continue  // ← Template 模式直接跳过了
    }
    claimName := *podClaim.ResourceClaimName
    // ...
}
```

Template 模式下的流程是：
1. Pod spec 引用 `ResourceClaimTemplateName`
2. K8s control plane 自动生成一个 ResourceClaim（名字为 `<pod-name>-<claim-name>-<random>`）
3. 生成的 claim 绑定在 `pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses` 中

两种处理方式：

| 方式 | 做法 | 优缺点 |
|------|------|--------|
| **A. 读 Template spec** | 通过 `ResourceClaimTemplateName` 查 template 对象，直接从 template.spec 提取请求 | 不依赖 claim 是否已生成；但需要额外 cache template 对象 |
| **B. 读生成的 claim** | 从 `pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses` 拿到生成的 claim name，再从 claimCache 查 | 不需要新 cache；但 pod status 可能还没更新（claim 未生成） |

我倾向于 **A + B 结合**：
- 优先从 `pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses` 查已生成的 claim（方式 B）
- 如果 claim 还没生成，fallback 到读 template spec（方式 A）
- 两者提取出的资源请求格式是一样的（都是 `devices.requests[]`）

这样既能处理 claim 已生成的稳态，也能处理 claim 尚未生成的调度早期阶段。

需要新增一个 ResourceClaimTemplate 的 cache/informer。当前 cache 中已有 `resourceClaimCache`，再加一个 `resourceClaimTemplateCache` 就行。

你觉得这个方案 OK 吗？

content.content:
# ResourceClaim Controller Key Logic

## 1. When ResourceClaims are Created

ResourceClaims are created from ResourceClaimTemplates when a Pod references a template through `podClaim.ResourceClaimTemplateName`. The controller's `handleClaim` function checks if a claim name exists; if not and a template is specified, it creates a new ResourceClaim.

## 2. Creation Triggers

Two main scenarios trigger creation:

- **Pod changes**: When a Pod is added or updated, `enqueuePod` processes it if it contains resource claims
- **Template changes**: When a ResourceClaimTemplate is modified, `enqueueResourceClaimTemplate` finds all Pods using that template and requeues them

## 3. Setting Generated Claim Name on Pod

The controller creates the ResourceClaim first, then updates the Pod's status through `ApplyStatus`:

```
"Patch the pod status with the new information about 
generated ResourceClaims"
```

It batches claim names in a map during processing and applies them as `PodResourceClaimStatus` entries.

## 4. Creation Timing: Before or After Scheduling

ResourceClaims can be created **before** pod scheduling. The code checks `if pod.Spec.NodeName == ""` to determine if scheduling hasn't occurred yet. The scheduler later handles reservations for already-scheduled pods.

## 5. Key Function Flow

- `syncPod()` → main handler
- `handleClaim()` → processes individual claims
- `findPodResourceClaim()` → checks for orphaned claims
- Template lookup → creates ResourceClaim with Pod as owner
- `ApplyStatus()` → updates Pod.Status.ResourceClaimStatuses

**结论：MVP 不需要额外工作，方案 B 天然支持。** 这也是方案 B 相比方案 A 的一个实际优势——方案 A 需要在 QueueStatus 新增 `DRAAllocated` 字段。

你同意直接复用现有 `status.allocated` 吗？

敲定：

> **QueueStatus.Allocated 直接复用，`deviceclass/` key 天然展示。** 无需额外工作。

---

## 问题 10：Webhook 校验

Queue 创建/更新时，`spec.capability` 中可能包含 `deviceclass/gpu.nvidia.com` 这样的 key。是否需要 admission webhook 校验？

几个校验维度：

| 校验项 | 是否需要 | 理由 |
|--------|---------|------|
| **key 格式合法性** | 建议做 | 验证 `deviceclass/` 和 `<dim>.deviceclass/` 格式正确，如 `deviceclass/` 后面不能为空 |
| **DeviceClass 是否存在** | 不建议 | DeviceClass 可能后于 Queue 创建（顺序不固定），强校验会导致部署顺序耦合 |
| **value 非负** | 建议做 | ResourceList 的 Quantity 不应为负数，现有校验可能已覆盖 |
| **capability >= guarantee** | 建议做 | `deviceclass/` key 应遵循与传统资源相同的约束 |
| **子队列 capability <= 父队列** | 取决于现有逻辑 | 如果现有 webhook 已做此校验，`deviceclass/` key 自动生效 |

让我看看当前 webhook 的校验逻辑：

content.content:
Found 2 files
pkg/webhooks/admission/queues/validate/validate_queue_test.go
pkg/webhooks/admission/queues/validate/validate_queue.go

content.text:
现有 webhook 已经很完整了。分析方案 B 的兼容性：

**现有校验对 `deviceclass/` key 的兼容情况：**

| 校验逻辑 | 位置 | 兼容性 |
|----------|------|--------|
| `ValidateResourceQuantityValue` — 值非负 | L231-233 | **自动兼容**，`deviceclass/` key 作为 ResourceName 传入，校验的是 Quantity 值 |
| `guarantee <= deserved` | L248-268 | **自动兼容**，按 ResourceName 逐 key 比较 |
| `deserved <= capability` | L271-290 | **自动兼容**，按 ResourceName 逐 key 比较 |
| 子队列 capability <= 祖先 capability | L574-588 | **自动兼容**，通过 `api.NewResource` + `getSingleResource` 走 ScalarResources 路径 |
| siblings guarantee/deserved 之和 <= 父队列 | L593-631 | **自动兼容**，`api.NewResource` + `Add` + `LessPartly` 都基于 ScalarResources map |

**结论：现有 webhook 校验逻辑完全兼容 `deviceclass/` key，不需要任何改动。** 这是方案 B 的又一个优势——因为 `deviceclass/` key 就是普通的 ResourceName，所有基于 ResourceList 的校验逻辑天然生效。

唯一可以考虑新增的是 **key 格式校验**（比如 `deviceclass/` 后不能为空），但 `ValidateResourceQuantityValue` 已经覆盖了值的合法性，key 格式不规范最多导致 capacity plugin 解析时 ignore，不会造成错误行为。

我建议 **MVP 不新增额外校验**，现有校验已足够。你同意吗？

敲定：

> **Webhook 不需要额外改动，现有校验天然兼容 `deviceclass/` key。**

---

到这里所有 10 个问题都讨论完了。让我做一个完整的最终汇总。

## 最终决策汇总

| # | 问题 | 决策 |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | K8s DRA API | `req.Exactly` 路径正确；`FirstAvailable` 跳过 quota，log warning |
| 2 | 方案选择 | **切到方案 B（`deviceclass/` 前缀）**，本次主要工作 |
| 3 | Shared ResourceClaim | 按 claim 去重，`resourceClaimRefs map[claimKey]sets.Set[TaskID]` |
| 4 | totalResource 来源 | ResourceSlice 汇总（跳过 partitionable devices）；root queue override 优先，不一致时 warning |
| 5 | Reclaim 释放延迟 | 直接扣减 quota，不等 claim 物理释放 |
| 6 | allocationMode: All | 跳过 quota 检查，log warning |
| 7 | share 计算 | DRA 维度正常参与，不特殊处理 |
| 8 | ResourceClaimTemplate | 从 `pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses` 获取生成的 claim 名，不需要 cache template |
| 9 | QueueStatus 展示 | 直接复用 `status.allocated`，`deviceclass/` key 天然展示 |
| 10 | Webhook 校验 | 不需要额外改动，现有校验天然兼容 |
| 命名 | 代码标识符 | 避免 `DRA` 前缀，用 `ResourceClaim`、`DeviceClass` 等实际语义 |

还有什么需要补充的吗？如果没有，我可以开始将这些决策更新到设计文档中。

1	# DRA Resource Quota Support in Capacity Plugin
2	
3	## Motivation
4	
5	The current Volcano capacity plugin manages CPU, Memory, and ScalarResources quotas. To support Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA), we need to extend the capacity plugin to manage DRA resource quotas (e.g., GPU, vGPU) for queues.
6	
7	DRA is a Kubernetes feature that provides a flexible way for requesting, configuring, and sharing specialized devices. It introduces new resource types (DeviceClass, ResourceClaim) that differ from traditional resources.
8	
9	## Goals
10	
11	1. Enable queue-level quota management for DRA resources
12	2. Track total device counts and optional capacity dimensions
13	3. Maintain consistency with existing queue quota semantics
14	4. Support hierarchical queues quota enforcement
15	
16	### Non-Goals
17	
18	- Managing physical device allocation (handled by DRA drivers and predicates plugin)
19	- Understanding DeviceClass semantics (capacity plugin only does numeric quota tracking)
20	
21	## Design Principles
22	
23	1. **Per-DeviceClass quota tracking**: Track quotas separately for each DeviceClass (e.g., nvidia-h100, hami-vgpu)
24	2. **Generic resource tracking**: Handle device count and optional capacity dimensions
25	3. **Follow existing Queue patterns**: Use similar structure to traditional resource quotas
26	4. **Support hierarchical queues** as an advanced feature. *(Note: Elastic preemption traits like `deserved` and `guarantee` for DRA resources are not supported by the capacity plugin directly and must be implemented in other plugins.)*
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Proposal
31	
32	### Feature Scenarios
33	
34	| Scenario | Capability | Prerequisites |
35	|----------|------------|---------------|
36	| **Basic** | DRA quota enforcement (`dra.capability`) | - |
37	| **Advanced** | Hierarchical queues (inheritance & constraints) | Basic |
38	
39	### Queue CRD Extension
40	
41	Add a new `dra` field to `QueueSpec`:
42	
43	```go
44	type QueueSpec struct {
45	    // ... existing fields (capability, deserved, guarantee) ...
46	    
47	    // DRA defines DRA resource quotas for the queue
48	    // +optional
49	    DRA *DRAQuota `json:"dra,omitempty"`
50	}
51	
52	// DRAQuota defines DRA resource quotas per DeviceClass
53	type DRAQuota struct {
54	    // Capability defines upper limit per DeviceClass (basic scenario)
55	    // Key: DeviceClass name (e.g., "nvidia-h100", "hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io")
56	    // +optional
57	    Capability map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"capability,omitempty"`
58	    // 
59	    // Note: Deserved and Guarantee fields are defined for future extensibility but are NOT 
60	    // actively used by the capacity plugin for elastic preemption today.
61	    
62	    // Deserved defines deserved quota per DeviceClass
63	    // +optional
64	    Deserved map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"deserved,omitempty"`
65	    
66	    // Guarantee defines minimum guaranteed quota per DeviceClass 
67	    // +optional
68	    Guarantee map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"guarantee,omitempty"`
69	}
70	
71	// DRAResourceQuota defines quota values for a specific DeviceClass
72	type DRAResourceQuota struct {
73	    // Count is the device count limit for this DeviceClass (required)
74	    Count int64 `json:"count"`
75	    
76	    // Capacity defines consumable capacity limits (optional)
77	    // Key: capacity dimension name (e.g., "cores", "memory")
78	    // This is used when DRA resources support consumable capacity
79	    // +optional
80	    Capacity map[string]resource.Quantity `json:"capacity,omitempty"`
81	}
82	```
83	
84	### Usage Example
85	
86	```yaml
87	apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
88	kind: Queue
89	metadata:
90	  name: ml-team
91	spec:
92	  # Traditional resource quota
93	  capability:
94	    cpu: "100"
95	    memory: "200Gi"
96	  
97	  # DRA resource quota (new)
98	  dra:
99	    # Basic: device count limit per DeviceClass
100	    capability:
101	      # Physical GPU quota
102	      nvidia-h100:
103	        count: 8
104	      # vGPU quota with capacity dimensions
105	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
106	        count: 80
107	        capacity:
108	          cores: "800"
109	          memory: "80Gi"
110	    # Note: Using deserved and guarantee currently has no effect on elasticity in the capacity plugin.
111	    # They may be utilized by other plugins in the future for DRA elasticity support.
112	    deserved:
113	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
114	        count: 40
115	    guarantee:
116	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
117	        count: 10
118	```
119	
120	### Quota Semantics
121	
122	| Field | Semantics | Enforcement |
123	|-------|-----------|-------------|
124	| `dra.capability` | Hard limit, cannot exceed | Allocation rejected if quota would be exceeded |
125	| `dra.deserved` | Future extensibility | Currenly NOT supported for elasticity/preemption in capacity plugin |
126	| `dra.guarantee` | Future extensibility | Currently NOT supported for elasticity/preemption in capacity plugin |
127	
128	---
129	
130	## Implementation Overview
131	
132	### Resource Aggregation
133	
134	The capacity plugin aggregates DRA requests from all ResourceClaims in a Pod, grouped by DeviceClass:
135	- Sum up device counts per DeviceClass across all claims
136	- Sum up capacity dimensions per DeviceClass across all claims
137	
138	Example:
139	```
140	Pod has 2 claims:
141	  Claim 1: DeviceClass=nvidia-h100, count=2
142	  Claim 2: DeviceClass=hami-core-gpu, count=1, cores=30, memory=4Gi
143	  Claim 3: DeviceClass=hami-core-gpu, count=1, cores=20, memory=2Gi
144	  
145	Aggregated request:
146	  nvidia-h100: {count=2}
147	  hami-core-gpu: {count=2, cores=50, memory=6Gi}
148	```
149	
150	### Quota Checking
151	
152	When scheduling a task:
153	1. For each DeviceClass in the task's DRA request:
154	   - Check if `allocated[deviceClass].count + request[deviceClass].count <= capability[deviceClass].count`
155	   - For each capacity dimension, check if `allocated[deviceClass][dim] + request[deviceClass][dim] <= capability[deviceClass][dim]`
156	2. If hierarchical queues enabled, recursively check parent queues
157	
158	### Relationship with Predicates Plugin
159	
160	| Component | Responsibility |
161	|-----------|----------------|
162	| **Capacity Plugin** | Queue quota management (logical limit) |
163	| **Predicates Plugin** | Node filtering, physical resource availability check |
164	
165	The two are decoupled:
166	- Capacity plugin ensures queue quota is not exceeded
167	- Predicates plugin (via K8s DRA) ensures nodes have sufficient physical resources
168	
169	---
170	
171	## Upgrade Strategy
172	
173	1. Add `dra` field to Queue CRD with `+optional` tag
174	2. Existing queues continue to work without modification
175	3. Scheduler gracefully handles queues without `dra` field (no DRA quota enforcement)
176	4. Users can gradually enable DRA quotas per queue

# DRA Resource Quota Support in Capacity Plugin

## Motivation

The current Volcano capacity plugin manages CPU, Memory, and ScalarResources quotas. To support Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA), we need to extend the capacity plugin to manage DRA resource quotas (e.g., GPU, vGPU) for queues.

DRA is a Kubernetes feature that provides a flexible way for requesting, configuring, and sharing specialized devices. It introduces new resource types (DeviceClass, ResourceClaim) that differ from traditional resources.

## Goals

1. Enable queue-level quota management for DRA resources
2. Track total device counts and optional capacity dimensions
3. Maintain consistency with existing queue quota semantics
4. Support hierarchical queues quota enforcement

### Non-Goals

- Managing physical device allocation (handled by DRA drivers and predicates plugin)
- Understanding DeviceClass semantics (capacity plugin only does numeric quota tracking)

## Design Principles

1. **Per-DeviceClass quota tracking**: Track quotas separately for each DeviceClass (e.g., nvidia-h100, hami-vgpu)
2. **Generic resource tracking**: Handle device count and optional capacity dimensions
3. **Follow existing Queue patterns**: Use similar structure to traditional resource quotas
4. **Support hierarchical queues** as an advanced feature. *(Note: Elastic preemption traits like `deserved` and `guarantee` for DRA resources are not supported by the capacity plugin directly and must be implemented in other plugins.)*

---

## Proposal

### Feature Scenarios

| Scenario | Capability | Prerequisites |
|----------|------------|---------------|
| **Basic** | DRA quota enforcement (`dra.capability`) | - |
| **Advanced** | Hierarchical queues (inheritance & constraints) | Basic |

### Queue CRD Extension

Add a new `dra` field to `QueueSpec`:

```go
type QueueSpec struct {
    // ... existing fields (capability, deserved, guarantee) ...
    
    // DRA defines DRA resource quotas for the queue
    // +optional
    DRA *DRAQuota `json:"dra,omitempty"`
}

// DRAQuota defines DRA resource quotas per DeviceClass
type DRAQuota struct {
    // Capability defines upper limit per DeviceClass (basic scenario)
    // Key: DeviceClass name (e.g., "nvidia-h100", "hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io")
    // +optional
    Capability map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"capability,omitempty"`
    // 
    // Note: Deserved and Guarantee fields are defined for future extensibility but are NOT 
    // actively used by the capacity plugin for elastic preemption today.
    
    // Deserved defines deserved quota per DeviceClass
    // +optional
    Deserved map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"deserved,omitempty"`
    
    // Guarantee defines minimum guaranteed quota per DeviceClass 
    // +optional
    Guarantee map[string]DRAResourceQuota `json:"guarantee,omitempty"`
}

// DRAResourceQuota defines quota values for a specific DeviceClass
type DRAResourceQuota struct {
    // Count is the device count limit for this DeviceClass (required)
    Count int64 `json:"count"`
    
    // Capacity defines consumable capacity limits (optional)
    // Key: capacity dimension name (e.g., "cores", "memory")
    // This is used when DRA resources support consumable capacity
    // +optional
    Capacity map[string]resource.Quantity `json:"capacity,omitempty"`
}
```

### Usage Example

```yaml
apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
kind: Queue
metadata:
  name: ml-team
spec:
  # Traditional resource quota
  capability:
    cpu: "100"
    memory: "200Gi"
  
  # DRA resource quota (new)
  dra:
    # Basic: device count limit per DeviceClass
    capability:
      # Physical GPU quota
      nvidia-h100:
        count: 8
      # vGPU quota with capacity dimensions
      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
        count: 80
        capacity:
          cores: "800"
          memory: "80Gi"
    # Note: Using deserved and guarantee currently has no effect on elasticity in the capacity plugin.
    # They may be utilized by other plugins in the future for DRA elasticity support.
    deserved:
      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
        count: 40
    guarantee:
      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
        count: 10
```

### Quota Semantics

| Field | Semantics | Enforcement |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `dra.capability` | Hard limit, cannot exceed | Allocation rejected if quota would be exceeded |
| `dra.deserved` | Future extensibility | Currenly NOT supported for elasticity/preemption in capacity plugin |
| `dra.guarantee` | Future extensibility | Currently NOT supported for elasticity/preemption in capacity plugin |

---

## Implementation Overview

### Resource Aggregation

The capacity plugin aggregates DRA requests from all ResourceClaims in a Pod, grouped by DeviceClass:
- Sum up device counts per DeviceClass across all claims
- Sum up capacity dimensions per DeviceClass across all claims

Example:
```
Pod has 2 claims:
  Claim 1: DeviceClass=nvidia-h100, count=2
  Claim 2: DeviceClass=hami-core-gpu, count=1, cores=30, memory=4Gi
  Claim 3: DeviceClass=hami-core-gpu, count=1, cores=20, memory=2Gi
  
Aggregated request:
  nvidia-h100: {count=2}
  hami-core-gpu: {count=2, cores=50, memory=6Gi}
```

### Quota Checking

When scheduling a task:
1. For each DeviceClass in the task's DRA request:
   - Check if `allocated[deviceClass].count + request[deviceClass].count <= capability[deviceClass].count`
   - For each capacity dimension, check if `allocated[deviceClass][dim] + request[deviceClass][dim] <= capability[deviceClass][dim]`
2. If hierarchical queues enabled, recursively check parent queues

### Relationship with Predicates Plugin

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|----------------|
| **Capacity Plugin** | Queue quota management (logical limit) |
| **Predicates Plugin** | Node filtering, physical resource availability check |

The two are decoupled:
- Capacity plugin ensures queue quota is not exceeded
- Predicates plugin (via K8s DRA) ensures nodes have sufficient physical resources

---

## Upgrade Strategy

1. Add `dra` field to Queue CRD with `+optional` tag
2. Existing queues continue to work without modification
3. Scheduler gracefully handles queues without `dra` field (no DRA quota enforcement)
4. Users can gradually enable DRA quotas per queue

## References

- [Kubernetes DRA Documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/dynamic-resource-allocation/)
- [DRA Consumable Capacity KEP](https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/5075-dra-consumable-capacity)
- [Volcano Capacity Plugin](./capacity-scheduling.md)
- [Volcano Hierarchical Queue](./hierarchical-queue-on-capacity-plugin.md)
- [Detailed Development Design](./capacity-dra-dev-design.md)

1	# DRA Quota User Guide
2	
3	## Introduction
4	
5	DRA (Dynamic Resource Allocation) Quota support allows you to manage specialized device resources (such as GPUs, vGPUs, or other DRA-enabled devices) at the queue level using the capacity plugin. This feature enables you to:
6	
7	- Set hard limits on DRA resources per DeviceClass for each queue
8	- Track device counts and optional capacity dimensions
9	- Support elastic resource sharing and reclamation between queues
10	
11	For design details, see [DRA Quota Design](../design/capacity-dra-support.md).
12	
13	## Prerequisites
14	
15	1. **Kubernetes Version**: v1.26 or later
16	2. **Feature Gates**: `DynamicResourceAllocation` must be enabled
17	3. **DRA Driver**: A DRA driver must be installed in your cluster (e.g., GPU driver with DRA support)
18	4. **Volcano**: Installed with capacity plugin enabled
19	
20	## Environment Setup
21	
22	### Install Volcano
23	
24	Refer to [Install Guide](https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano/blob/master/installer/README.md) to install Volcano.
25	
26	### Configure Scheduler
27	
28	Update the scheduler configuration to enable the capacity plugin:
29	
30	```shell
31	kubectl edit cm -n volcano-system volcano-scheduler-configmap
32	```
33	
34	Ensure:
35	- `reclaim` action is enabled
36	- `capacity` plugin is enabled (remove `proportion` plugin if present)
37	
38	```yaml
39	kind: ConfigMap
40	apiVersion: v1
41	metadata:
42	  name: volcano-scheduler-configmap
43	  namespace: volcano-system
44	data:
45	  volcano-scheduler.conf: |
46	    actions: "enqueue, allocate, backfill, reclaim"
47	    tiers:
48	    - plugins:
49	      - name: priority
50	      - name: gang
51	      - name: conformance
52	    - plugins:
53	      - name: drf
54	      - name: predicates
55	      - name: capacity  # Use capacity plugin
56	      - name: nodeorder
57	```
58	
59	### Install DRA Driver
60	
61	Install a DRA driver in your cluster. For example, if using GPU resources:
62	
63	```shell
64	# Example: Install a DRA-enabled GPU driver
65	kubectl apply -f https://example.com/gpu-dra-driver.yaml
66	```
67	
68	Verify that DeviceClass resources are available:
69	
70	```shell
71	kubectl get deviceclass
72	```
73	
74	## Configure Queue DRA Quotas
75	
76	### Basic Configuration
77	
78	Create a queue with DRA quota limits:
79	
80	```yaml
81	apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
82	kind: Queue
83	metadata:
84	  name: ml-team
85	spec:
86	  reclaimable: true
87	  # Traditional resource quota
88	  deserved:
89	    cpu: "100"
90	    memory: "200Gi"
91	  
92	  # DRA resource quota
93	  dra:
94	    capability:
95	      # GPU quota: maximum 8 GPUs
96	      gpu.example.com:
97	        count: 8
98	```
99	
100	### Advanced Configuration with Capacity Dimensions
101	
102	For DRA resources that support consumable capacity (e.g., vGPU with cores and memory):
103	
104	```yaml
105	apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
106	kind: Queue
107	metadata:
108	  name: ai-team
109	spec:
110	  reclaimable: true
111	  dra:
112	    capability:
113	      # vGPU quota with capacity dimensions
114	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
115	        count: 80
116	        capacity:
117	          cores: "800"
118	          memory: "80Gi"
119	    
120	    # Deserved quota (for elastic preemption)
121	    deserved:
122	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
123	        count: 40
124	        capacity:
125	          cores: "400"
126	          memory: "40Gi"
127	    
128	    # Guaranteed minimum (cannot be preempted)
129	    guarantee:
130	      hami-core-gpu.project-hami.io:
131	        count: 10
132	        capacity:
133	          cores: "100"
134	          memory: "10Gi"
135	```
136	
137	### Quota Semantics
138	
139	| Field | Meaning | Behavior |
140	|-------|---------|----------|
141	| `dra.capability` | Hard limit | Jobs exceeding this limit will remain pending |
142	| `dra.deserved` | Soft limit | Resources beyond deserved can be borrowed but may be reclaimed |
143	| `dra.guarantee` | Minimum guarantee | Resources within guarantee cannot be preempted |
144	
145	## Submit Jobs with DRA Resources
146	
147	### Create ResourceClaim
148	
149	First, create a ResourceClaim requesting DRA devices:
150	
151	```yaml
152	apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
153	kind: ResourceClaim
154	metadata:
155	  name: gpu-claim
156	  namespace: default
157	spec:
158	  devices:
159	    requests:
160	    - name: gpu-request
161	      exactly:
162	        deviceClassName: gpu.example.com
163	        count: 2
164	        allocationMode: ExactCount
165	```
166	
167	### Create Job Referencing ResourceClaim
168	
169	Create a Volcano Job that references the ResourceClaim:
170	
171	```yaml
172	apiVersion: batch.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
173	kind: Job
174	metadata:
175	  name: gpu-job
176	spec:
177	  schedulerName: volcano
178	  queue: ml-team
179	  minAvailable: 1
180	  tasks:
181	  - replicas: 1
182	    name: worker
183	    template:
184	      spec:
185	        containers:
186	        - name: main
187	          image: nvidia/cuda:11.0-base
188	          command: ["nvidia-smi"]
189	          resources:
190	            requests:
191	              cpu: "2"
192	              memory: "4Gi"
193	        resourceClaims:
194	        - name: gpu
195	          resourceClaimName: gpu-claim
196	```
197	
198	## Example Scenario: Multi-Queue Resource Sharing
199	
200	### Setup
201	
202	Assume you have:
203	- 2 queues: `queue-a` and `queue-b`
204	- Total cluster capacity: 8 GPUs (DeviceClass: `gpu.example.com`)
205	
206	Configure queues:
207	
208	```yaml
209	# Queue A: capability=4, deserved=2
210	apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
211	kind: Queue
212	metadata:
213	  name: queue-a
214	spec:
215	  reclaimable: true
216	  dra:
217	    capability:
218	      gpu.example.com:
219	        count: 4
220	    deserved:
221	      gpu.example.com:
222	        count: 2
223	---
224	# Queue B: capability=6, deserved=4
225	apiVersion: scheduling.volcano.sh/v1beta1
226	kind: Queue
227	metadata:
228	  name: queue-b
229	spec:
230	  reclaimable: true
231	  dra:
232	    capability:
233	      gpu.example.com:
234	        count: 6
235	    deserved:
236	      gpu.example.com:
237	        count: 4
238	```
239	
240	### Scenario Steps
241	
242	1. **Submit job to queue-a requesting 4 GPUs**
243	   - Result: Job runs successfully (queue-a can use up to 4 GPUs)
244	
245	2. **Submit job to queue-b requesting 4 GPUs**
246	   - Result: Queue-b reclaims its deserved resources (4 GPUs)
247	   - Queue-a's job is preempted down to its deserved quota (2 GPUs)
248	   - Queue-b's job gets 4 GPUs
249	
250	3. **Final state**:
251	   - Queue-a: 2 GPUs (deserved quota)
252	   - Queue-b: 4 GPUs (deserved quota)
253	   - 2 GPUs remain idle
254	
255	## Troubleshooting
256	
257	### Job Stays Pending
258	
259	**Symptom**: Job remains in Pending state
260	
261	**Possible Causes**:
262	1. DRA quota exceeded for the queue
263	2. No physical devices available on nodes
264	3. DeviceClass not configured in queue
265	
266	**Solution**:
267	```shell
268	# Check queue DRA quota
269	kubectl get queue <queue-name> -o yaml
270	
271	# Check job events
272	kubectl describe job <job-name>
273	
274	# Check ResourceClaim status
275	kubectl get resourceclaim <claim-name> -o yaml
276	```
277	
278	### Quota Not Enforced
279	
280	**Symptom**: Jobs exceed configured DRA quota
281	
282	**Possible Causes**:
283	1. Capacity plugin not enabled
284	2. Queue `dra` field not configured
285	
286	**Solution**:
287	```shell
288	# Verify capacity plugin is enabled
289	kubectl get cm -n volcano-system volcano-scheduler-configmap -o yaml
290	
291	# Verify queue configuration
292	kubectl get queue <queue-name> -o yaml
293	```
294	
295	## Best Practices
296	
297	1. **Set Realistic Quotas**: Ensure `capability` matches or is less than physical cluster capacity
298	2. **Use Deserved for Sharing**: Configure `deserved` to enable elastic resource sharing
299	3. **Guarantee Critical Workloads**: Use `guarantee` for production workloads that should not be preempted
300	4. **Monitor Usage**: Regularly check queue resource usage and adjust quotas as needed
301	
302	## References
303	
304	- [DRA Quota Design Document](../design/capacity-dra-support.md)
305	- [Capacity Plugin User Guide](./how_to_use_capacity_plugin.md)
306	- [Kubernetes DRA Documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/dynamic-resource-allocation/)
